steps.description: Identify top services contributing to latency in the last 2 hours.
Drill down into the dominant service to find its slowest endpoints.
Analyze spans for the slowest endpoint to identify the root cause (e.g., database, external calls).
If a database call is a bottleneck, investigate the slowest database statements.

dql: timeseries p95 = percentile(dt.service.request.response_time, 95, rollup: avg), by: { dt.service.name }, from: now()-2h | sort p95 desc | limit 10

fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and request.is_root_span == true | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95, rollup: avg), by: { endpoint.name } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and request.is_root_span == true | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { endpoint.name } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and endpoint.name == "POST /pay" | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { span.name, span.kind } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and endpoint.name == "POST /pay" and (span.kind == "client" or isNotNull(db.statement)) | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { span.name, span.kind, db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 10
fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { span.name, span.kind } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and span.name == "SELECT orders" and isNotNull(db.statement) | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

opportunity.recommendedAction: Add appropriate indexes to the 'email' and 'status' columns in the 'orders' table. Consider a composite index on (email, status, created_at) and refactoring the query to remove 'lower()' if not strictly necessary for performance.

evidence.resultSummary: checkout-service has a P95 response time of up to 4.47 seconds.
evidence.label: Checkout Service High Latency
evidence.dql: timeseries p95 = percentile(dt.service.request.response_time, 95, rollup: avg), by: { dt.service.name }, from: now()-2h | sort p95 desc | limit 10

evidence.label: POST /pay Slow Endpoint
evidence.dql: fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and request.is_root_span == true | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { endpoint.name } | sort p95 desc | limit 10
evidence.resultSummary: POST /pay endpoint in checkout-service has a P95 duration of ~4.2 seconds.
Database query 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE lower(email) = ? AND status <> 'failed' ORDER BY created_at' has a P95 duration of ~2.73 seconds.
evidence.label: Slow SELECT orders DB Query
evidence.dql: fetch spans, from: now()-2h | filter dt.service.name == "checkout-service" and span.name == "SELECT orders" and isNotNull(db.statement) | summarize p95 = percentile(duration, 95), by: { db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 10

opportunity.confidenceReason: Direct evidence from DQL showing a specific database query with a high P95 duration within the critical path of the slowest endpoint.

impact.metric: P95 response time of POST /pay endpoint
impact.assumption: The identified slow query contributes directly and dominantly to the 'POST /pay' endpoint's latency, and optimizing it will linearly reduce the endpoint's response time by the amount of reduction in query execution time.

opportunity.finding: The 'checkout-service's 'POST /pay' endpoint is significantly slowed by a database query 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE lower(email) = ? AND status <> 'failed' ORDER BY created_at', which has a P95 duration of ~2.73 seconds.
